results.label: Move server-1 from ToR1 to ToR2
Add new compute blade server-5 under ToR1
Add new compute blade server-6 under ToR2
CPU critical breach on server-1 (96%)
violations: Compute Overload on server-1: CPU=100.0% MEM=69.6% (Limit: 95.0%).
recommendations: Scale server-1 workload horizontally — add a sibling compute node under the same ToR switch and redistribute processes.

results.label: Memory critical breach on server-2 (96%)
violations: Compute Overload on server-2: CPU=57.4% MEM=100.0% (Limit: 95.0%).
recommendations: Scale server-2 workload horizontally — add a sibling compute node under the same ToR switch and redistribute processes.

results.label: Power envelope breach — high-draw workload on server-3 (420W)
violations: Compute Overload on server-3: CPU=98.4% MEM=76.5% (Limit: 95.0%).
recommendations: Scale server-3 workload horizontally — add a sibling compute node under the same ToR switch and redistribute processes.

results.label: Near-limit CPU that breaches on projected step 3 (88%)
violations: Compute Overload on server-1: CPU=100.0% MEM=58.0% (Limit: 95.0%).
recommendations: Scale server-1 workload horizontally — add a sibling compute node under the same ToR switch and redistribute processes.

results.label: Network latency warning on spine->router-1 (120ms)
violations: Network SLA Breach on spine-router→router-1: Latency=151.2ms (SLA Limit: 150.0ms).
recommendations: Inspect FRRouting BGP path on spine-router→router-1 — consider enabling ECMP load-balancing or switching to an alternate spine route.

results.label: Network SLA breach on spine->router-1 (160ms)
violations: Network SLA Breach on spine-router→router-1: Latency=201.6ms (SLA Limit: 150.0ms).
recommendations: Inspect FRRouting BGP path on spine-router→router-1 — consider enabling ECMP load-balancing or switching to an alternate spine route.

results.label: Packet loss breach on router-2->server-3 (7%)
violations: Packet Loss Breach on router-2→server-3: 11.20% loss.
recommendations: Check physical / virtual NIC on router-2→server-3 — packet loss indicates a flapping link or MTU mismatch in the Docker bridge network.

results.label: Combined latency + packet loss (200ms, 8%) on spine->router-2
violations: Network SLA Breach on spine-router→router-2: Latency=293.9ms (SLA Limit: 150.0ms).
Packet Loss Breach on spine-router→router-2: 16.00% loss.
recommendations: Inspect FRRouting BGP path on spine-router→router-2 — consider enabling ECMP load-balancing or switching to an alternate spine route.
Check physical / virtual NIC on spine-router→router-2 — packet loss indicates a flapping link or MTU mismatch in the Docker bridge network.

results.label: IOPS warning on server-1 (3100 IOPS)
violations: Storage IOPS Breach on server-1: 4650 IOPS (Limit: 4000 IOPS).
recommendations: Attach an additional NVMe volume to server-1 or enable read caching (Redis) to reduce raw disk IOPS pressure.

results.label: IOPS hard breach on server-2 (4050 IOPS)
violations: Storage IOPS Breach on server-2: 6075 IOPS (Limit: 4000 IOPS).
recommendations: Attach an additional NVMe volume to server-2 or enable read caching (Redis) to reduce raw disk IOPS pressure.

results.label: IOPS near-limit on neo4j (3800 IOPS) — full-scan workload
violations: Storage IOPS Breach on neo4j: 5700 IOPS (Limit: 4000 IOPS).
recommendations: Attach an additional NVMe volume to neo4j or enable read caching (Redis) to reduce raw disk IOPS pressure.

results.label: IOPS that projects past limit at step 2 (3500 IOPS base)
violations: Storage IOPS Breach on server-3: 5250 IOPS (Limit: 4000 IOPS).
recommendations: Attach an additional NVMe volume to server-3 or enable read caching (Redis) to reduce raw disk IOPS pressure.
